parsed.providerName: LaGuardia Community College - Adult & Continuing Education

checklistAnswers.question: Is the Transition program currently approved in the budget?
Has the individual already completed their educational program?
Does the program have published fees?
Is the program a noncredit-bearing transition program?
Does the coursework address the individual's valued outcome?
Does the coursework address skill-building and employment outcomes?
Are all staff/volunteers/trainers screened for criminal background & excluded-provider status? (letter)
Is the program provided in an OPWDD-certified location?
Can the program be funded by ACCES-VR, IDEA, or any other source?

parsed.justification: Baruch will build vocational and workplace-readiness skills toward competitive employment.
parsed.valuedOutcome: Baruch will build vocational and workplace-readiness skills toward competitive employment.

category.label: Transition Program
capNotes: Fee sanity-check: <= $350 per course / <= $800 per month.

webChecks.label: Published fees exist
webChecks.criteria: Concrete public pricing for the program/courses must be visible.

webChecks.label: Fees are within caps ($350/course, $800/month)
webChecks.criteria: Compare stated and published fees against the caps: $350 per course / $800 per month.

webChecks.label: Program is noncredit-bearing and oriented to skill-building / employment outcomes
webChecks.criteria: Look for 'non-credit', 'continuing education', 'workforce', 'job readiness', 'career skills' framing. Credit-bearing degree programs fail.

webChecks.label: Program is not in an OPWDD-certified location
webChecks.criteria: Check the venue: a community college or ordinary community venue is fine. If the program is delivered at an OPWDD-certified/provider-agency site, it fails. If the website does not say, mark Needs Review - do not guess.

webChecks.label: Staff background-screening (needs document, not website)
webChecks.criteria: This is proven by a letter from the provider, not the website. Always mark Needs Review with the note 'needs document: background-screening letter'.

webChecks.label: Published fee matches the fee stated on the form
webChecks.criteria: Compare the fee on the application to the published fee. Verdict: matches application exactly / differs / not published.

findings.note: The website lists various programs and courses but does not publish specific fees or tuition costs for them. It mentions "Affordable Programs" and scholarships but no concrete pricing information is available. [Auto-downgraded to Needs Review: the supporting quote could not be verified verbatim in the captured page text.]
findings.label: Published fees exist

findings.note: The website does not publish specific fees for courses or programs, so it is not possible to verify if published fees are within the stated caps. The fee on the form ($300 per course) is within the $350 per course cap.
findings.label: Fees are within caps ($350/course, $800/month)

findings.note: The "Continuing Education" division focuses on "Career Skills & Workforce Training" and explicitly offers "College Courses for Non-Credit", indicating a clear orientation towards skill-building and employment outcomes.
findings.quote: Continuing Education at LaGuardia Community College opens doors to opportunity. Whether you want to learn English, earn a high school equivalency diploma, go to college, get job training, begin or advance in a career, or grow your business, we can help you achieve your goals.
findings.label: Program is noncredit-bearing and oriented to skill-building / employment outcomes

findings.note: The program is offered at CUNY LaGuardia Community College, which is a public community college and not an OPWDD-certified location.
findings.quote: CUNY LaGuardia Community College 31-10 Thomson Avenue Long Island City, NY 11101 USA
findings.label: Program is not in an OPWDD-certified location

findings.note: Needs document: this item is proven by a provider letter, not the website. This check requires a separate background-screening letter from the provider, which is not available on the website.
findings.label: Staff background-screening (needs document, not website)

findings.note: The website does not publish specific fees for courses or programs, so a direct comparison to the stated fee of $300 per course on the application form cannot be made.
findings.label: Published fee matches the fee stated on the form

rateComparison.note: The website does not provide specific pricing for courses or programs, making it impossible to compare with the stated fee of $300 per course on the application form.

About Continuing Education

Continuing Education at LaGuardia Community College opens doors to opportunity. Whether you want to learn English, earn a high school equivalency diploma, go to college, get job training, begin or advance in a career, or grow your business, we can help you achieve your goals.

Expert Instructors
Supportive Classroom Environment
Diverse Student Body
Affordable Programs

Learn More
Read the Student Conduct Code 

Moving the Community Forward

HomeContinuing EducationCareer Skills & Workforce Training
Find Courses, Certificates & Programs
 
Request Info
 
Current Catalogs
Career Skills & Workforce Training

Do you want a meaningful job with a promising future?

Are you ready to take your career to the next level?

We offer a wide range of high quality, short term and affordable courses and certificate programs in most major industries to help you reach your goals.

Programs and courses are offered online or through a combination of online and in-person classes. Partial tuition scholarships are available for eligible students. A separate application process is required. 

Explore the many options available to start or continue your career path.

Healthcare
Advanced Industrial, Manufacturing & Transportation Programs
Technology
Film Production
Career Skills
Business & Finance
Construction, Building Management & Sustainability
Veterans Workforce Initiatives
Legal Interpreting
Education Training
Cybersecurity Training

Help protect against hackers! Cyber attacks are on the rise, and trained cybersecurity professionals are in high demand. Join thousands of experts who protect businesses, government agencies and general consumers.

Classes offered at our Manhattan location. Scholarships available.

Register Today

Certified Medical Administrative Assistant Program

Want to enter the healthcare field? Get training as a Certified Medical Administrative Assistant (CMAA) and earn over $46K (median salary). Our program offers career readiness and job placement support. Classes begin on April 1.

Scholarships are available!.

Register Today.

Healthcare Finance and Operations
(formerly Medical Billing)

Get hands-on training in medical billing and more to prepare for positions with large healthcare providers. Earn 9 college credits towards a Business Administration/Healthcare Management major at LaGuardia Community College.

Learn more.

QuickBooks Certificate Program

Learn QuickBooks, one of the most important accounting software programs! Begin a career in accounting or finance, or manage bookkeeping for your business.

Sign up for an information session!

Learn more.

Mental Health Career Pathway Initiative

Use your own lived experience with mental health challenges and recovery to become a peer support worker in the human services sector. Work in a variety of settings.

Learn more.

Become an Emergency Medical Technician

LaGuardia’s EMT Program trains NYC’s first responders.

HomeContinuing EducationPre-College and High School Equivalency
Find Courses, Certificates & Programs
 
Request Info
 
Current Catalogs
Pre-College and High School Equivalency

Preparing you to earn your high school equivalency diploma, getting you into college and putting you on the path to a vibrant career and brighter future is what LaGuardia Community College does for thousands of students each year. Whether you need to work on basic skills or want support as you transition into college studies, our wide variety of programs meet you at the level you’re at and help you reach your educational goals.

Our pioneering programs are making an impact regionally and nationally, and have become models for other institutes disseminated through educator training across the country.

High School Equivalence (HSE)
Pre-College Programs
Youth Programs
College Courses for Non-Credit
Adult Basic Skills
Improve your earning potential by getting a high school equivalency diploma. Study in a supportive learning environment.
College Modern Languages

Learn a new language! Continuing Education at LaGuardia offers college level Modern Language courses on a non-credit basis. Choose from Spanish, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Arabic and more. Elementary and some advanced level courses are available.

Register now for an upcoming course!

CUNY Start

Start out strong in your college career and reduce the amount of time spent in required remedial classes.

CUNY Fatherhood Academy
If you are a father between the ages of 18 and 30, unemployed or underemployed, we can help you create a brighter future for you and your family.
